1.title: Called and Greeted by Grace
1.summary: Paul greets God’s church in Corinth with grace and peace from the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

1.title: Comfort That Overflows
1.summary: God comforts his servants in affliction so that his comfort can flow through them to others.

1.title: Delivered Through Prayer
1.summary: A deadly trial teaches Paul to rely on the God who raises the dead and answers united prayer.

1.title: A Clear Conscience
1.summary: Paul appeals to the sincerity and godly simplicity that have governed his conduct toward the Corinthians.

1.title: God’s Faithfulness and Paul’s Changed Plans
1.summary: Paul explains his altered visit by pointing to God’s unwavering Yes in Christ and his desire to spare the church further pain.

2.title: A Painful Decision Written in Love
2.summary: Paul delayed another visit and wrote through tears because he wanted correction to reveal the depth of his love.

2.title: Forgive and Restore the Offender
2.summary: The congregation must forgive, comfort, and restore the repentant person so that sorrow and Satan do not gain an advantage.

2.title: An Open Door, an Anxious Heart
2.summary: Although ministry opened in Troas, Paul’s concern for Corinth moved him onward when he could not find Titus.

2.title: The Fragrance of Christ
2.summary: God leads his servants in Christ’s triumph and spreads the knowledge of Christ through sincere gospel ministry.

3.title: Living Letters from Christ
3.summary: The transformed Corinthians are Christ’s public letter, written by the Spirit rather than with ink.

3.title: Competent Ministers of the New Covenant
3.summary: Paul’s confidence rests in God, who makes servants able to minister the life-giving covenant of the Spirit.

3.title: A Glory That Surpasses
3.summary: The lasting ministry of righteousness carries a glory greater than the fading glory of the former covenant.

3.title: Unveiled Faces, Transforming Glory
3.summary: In Christ the veil is removed, and the Spirit transforms believers as they behold the Lord’s glory.

4.title: The Light of the Gospel
4.summary: Paul rejects hidden deceit and openly proclaims Christ, through whom God shines his light into human hearts.

4.title: Treasure in Fragile Jars
4.summary: God places gospel treasure in weak servants so that sustaining power is clearly his and Jesus’ life is revealed through suffering.

4.title: Faith That Speaks
4.summary: Resurrection faith enables Paul to speak and endure so that grace reaches more people and multiplies thanksgiving.

4.title: Eternal Glory Beyond Sight
4.summary: Present affliction renews hope as believers look beyond visible troubles to eternal glory.

5.title: Clothed with Heavenly Life
5.summary: Believers long for resurrection life, and God gives the Spirit as the guarantee of what is coming.

5.title: Walking by Faith, Pleasing Christ
5.summary: Confidence in the Lord leads believers to walk by faith and live in view of Christ’s judgment seat.

5.title: Christ’s Love Compels Us
5.summary: Christ’s death and resurrection reshape the purpose of life and compel his servants to persuade others.

5.title: New Creation and Reconciliation
5.summary: God creates a new people in Christ and entrusts them with the message that calls the world to reconciliation with him.

6.title: Now Is the Day of Salvation
6.summary: Paul urges the Corinthians not to receive grace in vain because God’s saving moment is now.

6.title: The Marks of Faithful Ministry
6.summary: Patient endurance, purity, truth, and divine power commend God’s servants through every reversal and hardship.

6.title: Open Your Hearts Wide
6.summary: Paul answers the Corinthians with candid affection and asks them to make equal room for him in their hearts.

6.title: Set Apart for the Living God
6.summary: God’s temple must not be joined to idolatry, for his people belong to him as sons and daughters.

7.title: Completing Holiness
7.summary: God’s promises call believers to cleanse their lives and pursue holiness in reverent fear.

7.title: Make Room in Your Hearts
7.summary: Paul renews his appeal for openhearted fellowship and expresses confidence and joy in the Corinthians.

7.title: Comforted by Titus
7.summary: God comforts Paul’s troubled heart through Titus and the welcome news he brings from Corinth.

7.title: Sorrow That Leads to Repentance
7.summary: The painful letter produced godly sorrow, earnest repentance, and a renewed desire to do what was right.

7.title: Joy and Restored Confidence
7.summary: Titus’s refreshed spirit and the Corinthians’ response deepen Paul’s comfort, joy, and trust in them.

8.title: Grace-Filled Generosity in Macedonia
8.summary: The Macedonian believers give beyond expectation because they first give themselves to the Lord.

8.title: Complete This Work of Grace
8.summary: Paul asks Corinth to excel in giving and points to Christ’s self-giving grace as its supreme pattern.

8.title: Willing Gifts and Fair Sharing
8.summary: The church should complete what it began, giving willingly from what it has so that needs are met fairly.

8.title: Trusted Partners and Careful Stewardship
8.summary: Titus and the appointed brothers handle the gift openly so that the churches can see both integrity and love.

9.title: Prepare the Gift in Advance
9.summary: Paul sends the brothers ahead so that Corinth’s promised gift will be ready as a willing blessing rather than a forced demand.

9.title: Sow Generously, Give Cheerfully
9.summary: God supplies generous givers with what they need to abound in good works and extend generosity to others.

9.title: Giving That Overflows in Thanksgiving
9.summary: The collection meets practical needs, strengthens fellowship, and causes many people to thank God for his surpassing gift.

10.title: Spiritual Weapons for Obedience
10.summary: Paul confronts proud resistance with divine weapons that demolish false arguments and bring thoughts under Christ’s rule.

10.title: Authority to Build Up
10.summary: Paul defends the consistency of his presence and letters while insisting that the Lord gave authority for the church’s good.

10.title: The Measure God Assigns
10.summary: True ministry stays within God’s calling and seeks the Lord’s approval instead of boasting through human comparison.

11.title: Godly Jealousy for a Pure Gospel
11.summary: Paul fears that the Corinthians may be drawn from simple devotion to Christ by another Jesus, spirit, or gospel.

11.title: False Apostles Unmasked
11.summary: Paul’s refusal to burden Corinth contrasts with deceitful workers who disguise themselves as servants of righteousness.

11.title: Bear with My Foolish Boasting
11.summary: Paul reluctantly adopts the language of boasting to expose the church’s tolerance of domineering pretenders.

11.title: A Servant Marked by Suffering
11.summary: Paul’s labor, danger, weakness, and daily care for the churches reveal the costly shape of his service.

11.title: Boasting in Weakness
11.summary: Paul closes his boast with weakness, recalling his humiliating escape from Damascus under threat.

12.title: Visions and Revelations
12.summary: Paul recounts an extraordinary heavenly experience without using it to exalt himself above what others can see and hear.

12.title: Power Perfected in Weakness
12.summary: The thorn keeps Paul humble and teaches him to rest in Christ’s grace, whose power is perfected in weakness.

12.title: The Signs of a True Apostle
12.summary: Paul reminds the Corinthians that patient ministry and mighty works among them confirmed his apostolic calling.

12.title: Spending Himself, Not Their Money
12.summary: Preparing for a third visit, Paul seeks the Corinthians themselves and shows that his coworkers shared the same integrity.

12.title: Preparing the Church for His Visit
12.summary: Paul speaks for their upbuilding while warning that unresolved conflict and impurity may bring grief when he arrives.

13.title: A Final Warning Before the Third Visit
13.summary: Paul warns that persistent sin will be addressed by the power of the risen Christ when he comes again.

13.title: Examine Yourselves and Be Restored
13.summary: The Corinthians must test themselves, pursue maturity, and receive Paul’s authority as a means of building up.

13.title: Final Appeal and Trinitarian Blessing
13.summary: Paul closes with a call to unity and peace and blesses the church with Christ’s grace, God’s love, and the Spirit’s fellowship.
